title: senml: skip_one_depth doesn't handle indefinite-length CBOR
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 363-377 (skip_one_depth)

The skip_one_depth function only handles definite-length arrays and maps. CBOR supports indefinite-length encoding (info = 31, 0x1f), which is terminated by a break code (0xff).

Current behavior: info >= 26 returns InvalidInput, which includes:
- info 26 (32-bit length) - valid but unsupported
- info 27 (64-bit length) - valid but unsupported  
- info 28-30 - reserved/invalid
- info 31 (0x1f) - indefinite length - valid but unsupported

For SenML specifically, indefinite-length encoding is rare but legal CBOR. A conformant encoder could produce indefinite-length arrays or maps.

Impact: Decoder rejects valid CBOR using indefinite-length encoding.

Fix: Either:
1. Explicitly handle info 31 to return NotImplemented (clearer error)
2. Implement indefinite-length parsing (more complete)

Minimal fix:
```rust
match info {
    0..=23 => (info as u64, 1),
    24 => ...
    25 => ...
    26 | 27 => return Err(CborError::NotImplemented),
    31 => return Err(CborError::NotImplemented),  // indefinite
    _ => return Err(CborError::InvalidInput),
}
```
close_reason: Fixed per codereview: cbor.rs indefinite->NotImplemented + name concat len check (kiab/sciu), codec.rs finalize zero->0xFFFF (mqoh), app_identity.c copy_string single strlen + removed redundant memset (0bb7/2jco). 3 subagent passes (explore+2 general reviews), cargo test -p lichen-senml/schc pass, no test weakening, RustCrypto/C conventions followed. Epic complete.
